prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a thorp has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the thorp-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a thorp, a snig, and a zop. The thorp is underneath the snig. The snig is to-the-left-of the zop. The thorp has a green button on top, a purple button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The snig has a purple button on top and a green button on bottom. The zop has a blue button on top and a green button on side. Pressing the green button on the thorp activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a clop, a klon, and a yup. The klon is underneath the yup. The yup is to-the-left-of the clop. The clop has a blue button on top and a purple button on bottom. The klon has a green button on top, a purple button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The yup has a green button on top, a green button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The yup's green button is the one usually associated with activation in similar objects.

Which object in the perception scenario is the thorp-analog, and which button activates it?
